controls.details: Vulnerability scanning is performed weekly using Trivy for container images and Dependabot for dependencies. Results are reviewed by the security team and tracked in GitHub Issues.
Default configuration hardened: debug mode disabled, CORS restricted to known origins, security headers (HSTS, X-Frame-Options) added. Ready for security review.
Application checks for updates every 24 hours via a scheduled CI/CD pipeline. Critical patches are deployed within 48 hours of release.
Automatic updates are enabled for the backend services. Frontend auto-update mechanism is still being evaluated — considering a feature flag approach.
Users can postpone updates and receive notifications. Implementation pending — design document drafted, awaiting approval.
Role-based access control (RBAC) is implemented for all API endpoints. JWT-based authentication with refresh tokens. Some legacy endpoints still need authorization checks backported.
Password policy enforces minimum 12 characters, complexity requirements, and rate limiting. Argon2id hashing with pepper. Password rotation every 90 days.
Unauthorized access attempts are logged and monitored. Automated alerts trigger after 5 failed attempts. Incidents are escalated to the security team via PagerDuty.
MFA enforced for all admin accounts using TOTP. SMS fallback available but discouraged. Recovery codes generated with proper entropy. Awaiting final security review.
Anomaly detection integrated via AWS GuardDuty for infrastructure layer. Application-level anomaly detection using session behavior analysis is under development.
AES-256-GCM encryption at rest for all sensitive data fields. Database-level TDE is enabled. Key rotation happens quarterly via AWS KMS.
Cryptographic algorithms reviewed and validated against NIST recommendations. RSA-2048+ for asymmetric, SHA-256+ for hashing. Legacy SHA-1 usage eliminated.
TLS 1.3 enforced for all external traffic. Internal service-to-service communication uses mTLS via a service mesh. HSTS preload enabled. Awaiting review from infrastructure team.
All external communication uses TLS 1.3 with strong cipher suites. SSH access restricted to ED25519 keys. FTP and other insecure protocols disabled.
Password policy enforcement verified across all authentication flows. Failed attempts trigger lockout after 5 tries. Account recovery uses verified email + security questions.
Data minimization principles applied to user registration — only essential fields collected. API responses reviewed to remove unnecessary PII. Still working on legacy data cleanup.
Automated data deletion for inactive accounts after 24 months is planned. GDPR retention policy drafted but not yet implemented. Scheduled for next sprint.
AWS Shield Advanced enabled for DDoS protection. Web Application Firewall (AWS WAF) configured with rate-limiting rules. Traffic monitoring via CloudWatch dashboards.
Complete API surface inventory maintained in OpenAPI 3.1 spec. All endpoints documented with authentication requirements. Reviewed quarterly for deprecated endpoints.
Risk assessment completed mapping all services to CRA controls. Each service categorized by criticality. Mitigation plan documented for identified gaps. Awaiting management sign-off.
Attack surface analysis in progress. External attack surface mapped using Amass. Internal attack surface mapping 60% complete. Remediation of 3 high-risk findings underway.
Automated daily backups of all databases to encrypted S3 buckets with 30-day retention. Quarterly restore tests verify backup integrity. Cross-region replication enabled.
Audit logging is implemented for authentication events, data access, and admin actions. Logs are shipped to a centralized SIEM. Still working on adding logging for configuration changes.
All log entries include actor identity, timestamp, and source IP. Session correlation IDs enable tracing user activity across services. PII is masked in logs per GDPR.
Logging opt-out mechanism implemented for user activity tracking. Privacy dashboard allows users to view and delete their activity data. Awaiting legal review.
Security-relevant data (incidents, vulnerabilities, compliance status) is accessible to users via a self-service portal. Role-based visibility ensures appropriate access.
Secure data deletion procedure implemented and verified. Data is securely wiped using DoD 5220.22-M standard. Deletion is logged and verified by a second reviewer.
